title: hieracium godbyense
parts: Hieracium godbyense has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, flowers, and roots, which possess various constituents with potential health benefits. The leaves of Hieracium godbyense contain sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of this plant are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to their antimicrobial and antitumor activities. The roots of Hieracium godbyense contain sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress.
side_effects: Hieracium godbyense can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. In severe cases, excessive consumption may lead to allergic reactions, which can manifest as skin irritation, hives, and respiratory problems. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can also cause liver and kidney damage, increasing the risk of liver and kidney failure. It is essential to handle and consume Hieracium godbyense with caution, as improper use can result in severe and potentially life-threatening health consequences.
